grp: Part 7 — Questions 176-180 refer to the following press release and review.
passage:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
SYDNEY (4 June) — Kitchen Swifts and Chef Darius Cordero are joining together to give home cooks a new culinary experience. The award-winning chef is the owner of restaurants in both the Philippines and Australia, including the recently opened Enriqua's. He says his cooking reflects his Filipino heritage, which is a blend of many cultures.

"I've designed these simplified recipes for Kitchen Swifts so that cooks at home can enjoy new and exciting flavours with ease," he said.

Zahra Chambers, vice president of Kitchen Swifts, says she is pleased to work with Chef Cordero and to offer delicious new recipes to their customers. Kitchen Swifts supplies menus, recipes, and ingredients for two people, four people, or six people, including a range of vegetarian selections. Customers choose the most appropriate meal options, and then a box is delivered weekly. Current customers will see no price increase with the partnership.

---
REVIEW: A colleague arranged for us to eat at Enriqua's while I was at a conference in Sydney. It is usually fully booked for dinner; you may need to call months in advance for a table. We had a wonderful lunch there instead. Everything was delicious, and the bread and desserts are baked on-site! It was a worthwhile treat before I flew back to Hong Kong.
— Meili Guan
text: 176. What is the purpose of the press release?
opts: (A) To promote the opening of a restaurant

From: Conor Boyle <cboyle@ceoleire.co.uk>
To: Laura Savard <laura.savard@orbitmail.scot>
Date: 25 May
Subject: RE: Some suggestions

Dear Ms. Savard,

Thank you for your kind offer to either pick up your online order from my shop or to pay extra for air or train transport. Neither arrangement is necessary, as I am happy to deliver your items to you in Stranraer myself. It so happens that my sister and her children live nearby in Kirkcolm. Before seeing them, I will drive my rental car to your house and hand deliver the items to you.

As you know, my merchandise is 100 percent handcrafted. If any damage occurs in transit, the repair turns into an expensive, time-consuming ordeal. I look forward to meeting you on 5 June. I expect to arrive at your house no later than 5 p.m.

Sincerely,
Conor Boyle, Ceoleire Classics

grp: Part 7 — Questions 191-195 refer to the following article, review, and e-mail.
passage:
ARTICLE — Caribbean Flavours Abound by Rebecca Roats
NOTTINGHAM — Orange Bay Kitchen has been serving up an infusion of Jamaican flavours in a laid-back Caribbean atmosphere for six months now. Managed by Keron Deslandes, the 150-seat restaurant is an aromatic jewel amid the bustling shops and eateries in Wester Square. The restaurant is most famous for its jerk chicken. Marinated for 24 hours prior to grilling and served with sides of stewed cabbage and coconut rice, the dish is a good deal at €12. If you stop in on any Friday night between 7 and 11 P.M., you will enjoy live reggae music.

---
REVIEW (22 August) — Tamika Peterkin: Orange Bay Kitchen: 2/5 Stars
After reading a glowing article about Orange Bay Kitchen, I was eager to give this place a try. My husband and I arrived there at 7 P.M. yesterday, keen to enjoy live music with our dinner. Unfortunately, the band's performance that night had been cancelled. We stayed and both ordered the jerk chicken. While the chicken's smoky flavour was outstanding, the stewed cabbage was lacking in flavour. The head chef came out to apologise and was extremely nice, but we will probably not go back anytime soon.

---
E-MAIL (24 August) — From: Vea Smith, Owner, Orange Bay Kitchen
Dear Ms. Peterkin,
Thank you for visiting Orange Bay Kitchen and leaving a review. Our manager, Keron Deslandes, told me more about your visit. Please accept the attached €20 gift certificate. During your visit, our band had an equipment malfunction. However, the band will be back performing weekly beginning in September. Also, Head Chef Adio Brown has changed the spices he uses in the stewed cabbage.
Sincerely, Vea Smith
text: 191. What does the article mention about Orange Bay Kitchen?
opts: (B) It is located on a quiet street.
(C) It has another location in Jamaica.
